notes: SOC2 risk assessment artifacts directly satisfy HIPAA risk analysis requirement. Evidence fully reusable.
SOC2 risk treatment and mitigation documentation covers HIPAA risk management requirements.
SOC2 code of conduct and HR policies cover general workforce sanctions. HIPAA-specific language must be added.
SOC2 log monitoring and review procedures directly satisfy HIPAA activity review requirements.
SOC2 security responsibility assignment covers HIPAA Security Officer designation requirement.
SOC2 access provisioning covers authorization; ePHI-specific supervision procedures needed.
SOC2 background check procedures overlap. HIPAA requires ePHI-specific access appropriateness review.
SOC2 offboarding and access revocation procedures fully cover HIPAA termination requirements.
SOC2 logical access controls and authorization documentation satisfy HIPAA access management.
SOC2 access provisioning and periodic review processes fully cover HIPAA access management lifecycle.
SOC2 security awareness program covers reminders. HIPAA-specific ePHI handling content must be included.
SOC2 malware protection controls fully satisfy HIPAA malicious software protection requirement.
SOC2 anomaly detection and login monitoring directly covers HIPAA log-in monitoring.
SOC2 password policy and MFA requirements satisfy HIPAA password management controls.
SOC2 incident response procedures cover identification and response. HIPAA adds breach notification requirements.
SOC2 availability criteria backup requirements fully satisfy HIPAA data backup plan.
SOC2 disaster recovery planning and testing documentation covers HIPAA DR plan requirements.
SOC2 business continuity documentation covers HIPAA emergency mode operation requirements.
SOC2 BCP testing covers the testing requirement. HIPAA requires documented ePHI-specific recovery validation.
SOC2 risk assessment includes system criticality. HIPAA requires explicit ePHI data classification layer.
SOC2 monitoring and evaluation activities satisfy HIPAA periodic evaluation requirement.
No SOC2 equivalent. BAA management is HIPAA-specific. Vendor management in SOC2 does not cover ePHI disclosure agreements.
SOC2 security awareness training overlaps. Must add HIPAA-specific modules on ePHI handling and breach reporting.
SOC2 incident response evaluation overlaps with the assessment workflow. HIPAA requires the four factor analysis specific to PHI compromise probability.
No SOC2 equivalent. Individual breach notification content, timing, and delivery are HIPAA-specific obligations.

hipaa_spec: Media Notice for Breaches Affecting 500 or More
notes: No SOC2 equivalent. Media notification thresholds and timing are HIPAA-specific.
No SOC2 equivalent. Submission to the HHS Secretary via the OCR portal is a HIPAA-specific obligation.
SOC2 vendor management covers communication of incidents. HIPAA requires the specific 60 day BA to CE notification with affected individual identifiers.
SOC2 incident documentation overlaps. HIPAA requires the six year retention plus documented basis for any non breach determination.
SOC2 recovery and incident response plans overlap. HIPAA requires breach specific roles, four factor decision criteria, and notification ownership.
SOC2 physical access controls partially cover emergency facility access. ePHI recovery procedures need documentation.
SOC2 physical security documentation covers the plan requirement. HIPAA needs ePHI-specific facility controls.
SOC2 physical access validation overlaps. Role-based physical access documentation should reference ePHI areas.
No direct SOC2 equivalent. Physical maintenance logs for ePHI systems are HIPAA-specific.
SOC2 endpoint controls and acceptable use policies satisfy HIPAA workstation use requirement.
SOC2 endpoint security configuration fully covers HIPAA workstation security controls.
SOC2 data disposal and media sanitization policies directly satisfy HIPAA disposal requirement.
SOC2 media handling procedures cover HIPAA re-use sanitization requirement.
SOC2 does not require hardware movement tracking. Asset inventory partially helps but is not equivalent.
SOC2 backup procedures cover the backup-before-move requirement for ePHI media.
No SOC2 equivalent. The Notice of Privacy Practices is a Privacy Rule artifact specific to HIPAA covered entities.
No SOC2 equivalent. The 30 day individual access right is a Privacy Rule obligation.
No SOC2 equivalent. Patient amendment rights are Privacy Rule specific.
No SOC2 equivalent. The six year disclosure accounting is a Privacy Rule artifact.
No SOC2 equivalent. Restriction requests are a Privacy Rule individual right.
No SOC2 equivalent. Alternate channel communication accommodations are Privacy Rule specific.
SOC2 logical access and least privilege overlap. HIPAA additionally requires minimum necessary standards for routine disclosures of PHI.
No SOC2 equivalent. Patient authorization content and validity rules are Privacy Rule specific.
No SOC2 equivalent. Treatment, payment, healthcare operations, and listed public interest categories are Privacy Rule constructs.
No SOC2 equivalent. Marketing authorization with remuneration disclosure is Privacy Rule specific.
No SOC2 equivalent. Sale of PHI authorization is Privacy Rule specific.
SOC2 designates a security owner; the Privacy Officer is a separate Privacy Rule role. No reusable evidence.
SOC2 commitment to competence overlaps with workforce training cadence. HIPAA additionally requires Privacy Rule specific curriculum and retraining on material changes.
SOC2 identity and access management controls fully satisfy HIPAA unique user ID requirement.
SOC2 availability planning partially covers emergency access. Break-glass procedures must be ePHI-specific.
SOC2 session management controls satisfy HIPAA automatic logoff requirement.
SOC2 data protection controls covering encryption at rest fully satisfy this HIPAA addressable spec.
SOC2 audit logging requirements are comprehensive and fully satisfy HIPAA audit controls.
SOC2 data integrity controls partially cover ePHI integrity verification. Hash/checksum mechanisms need documentation.
SOC2 MFA and authentication controls fully cover HIPAA entity authentication requirement.
SOC2 transmission security controls satisfy HIPAA in-transit integrity controls.
SOC2 encryption in transit requirements fully satisfy HIPAA transmission encryption addressable spec.
